title: Publications
text:
Published papers

K. Maciejowska, A. Lipiecki, B. Uniejewski. Statistical and economic evaluation of forecasts in electricity markets: Beyond RMSE and MAE. Energy Conversion and Management 356, 121408 (2026). 10.1016/j.enconman.2026.121408
A. Lipiecki, K. Sznajd-Weron. When heterogeneity drives hysteresis: Anticonformity in the multistate q -voter model on networks. Physical Review E 112(5), 054316 (2025). 10.1103/9f3r-fsrr
A. Lipiecki, R. Weron. PostForecasts.jl: A Julia package for probabilistic forecasting by postprocessing point predictions. SoftwareX 31, 102200 (2025). 10.1016/j.softx.2025.102200
A. Lipiecki, K. Sznajd-Weron. Depolarizing power of anticonformity. Expert Systems with Applications 285, 127879 (2025). 10.1016/j.eswa.2025.127879
A. Lipiecki, B. Uniejewski, R. Weron. Postprocessing of point predictions for probabilistic forecasting of day-ahead electricity prices: The benefits of using isotonic distributional regression. Energy Economics 139, 107934 (2024). 10.1016/j.eneco.2024.107934

Lecture Notes in Computer Science (including subseries Lecture Notes in Artificial Intelligence and Lecture Notes in Bioinformatics) 14834 LNCS, 62–70 (2024). 10.1007/978-3-031-63759-9_8

M. Doniec, A. Lipiecki, K. Sznajd-Weron. Consensus, Polarization and Hysteresis in the Three-State Noisy q-Voter Model with Bounded Confidence. Entropy 24(7), 983 (2022). 10.3390/e24070983
A. Abramiuk-Szurlej, A. Lipiecki, J. Pawłowski, K. Sznajd-Weron. Discontinuous phase transitions in the q-voter model with generalized anticonformity on random graphs. Scientific Reports 11(1), 17719 (2021). 10.1038/s41598-021-97155-0

title: Arkadiusz Lipiecki
text:
Hi, my name is Arek, I’m currently pursuing a PhD in Economics at Wrocław Tech. My research focuses on energy forecasting and social simulation. I hold a BSc in Quantum Engineering and an MSc in Big Data Analytics.
Right now, I’m mostly invested in research on probabilistic forecasting and reconciliation for day-ahead electricity markets using statistical methods and deep learning models. I also study opinion dynamics and emergent social phenomena through the lens of agent-based models and dynamical systems, with a particular interest in polarization and anticonformity.

title: Software
text:
lassoquantreg
A simple Python package based on scikit-learn and optuna that provides automatic selection of the best lasso quantile regression model.
pip install git+https://github.com/lipiecki/lassoquantreg.git

title: Software
text:
QVoterSeeding.jl
Julia package for network seeding simulations under q-voter dynamics.
pkg> add https://github.com/lipiecki/QVoterSeeding.jl
